item.title: Somber
item.content:encoded: Damn this is really REALLY good! It was sad, and you plucked at a heartstring or two. I wasn't bored one-bit reading this thing. Although, I noticed a typo. When SCP-05 shows a lion running through the plains, you wrote: "shot by hours". Looks like you meant to put "Arrows"? whatever. Excellent article. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to disagree with you here. Removing the table would really impede on explaining what the actual SCP is and what it does, and it is described fairly well. I at least was fairly engaged until the interviews, which are the real "meat" of the article.

Also note that this is, at its heart, an article, not necessarily a tale, so some narrative flow has to be sacrificed to actually describe the anomaly itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to be honest, after the 50th or so word I couldn't pronounce, my eyes started to glaze over a bit. This is very dense with history and theology that I am, and I imagine a lot of others are, unfamiliar with, which makes it pretty difficult to read. I kind of feel like you could cut the entire table out of this, or at least trim it way down, without losing much, as the interview logs are clearly the meat of what's going on here.

I think if this got to the end quicker I would have upvoted, but as it is I had to force myself to keep going - I'm clearly not the audience for this one.

minor issue
– hide block

I spotted one minor issue which I correct but I'm not sure what it's meant to be here:

The lion is shot by several hours; it begins to bleed onto the path.

If "hours" is correct here, I can't parse this sentence at all, heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That is a very good point- will alter.

Glad you liked it :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good and cool - I Love Gnosticism. Only small complaint I had is the use of "SCP logo" in the last interview log - maybe "Foundation logo" or "Foundation seal"? An easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And the fascists have the outfits
But I don't care for the outfits
What I care about is music
And the communists have the music

With thanks to Uncle Nicolini for critique.

The research and a lot of the ideas here lean a lot on Kathryn Babayan's excellent work Mystics, Monarchs and Messiahs, which I highly recommend if you're interested in all this stuff. I'd also recommend the historical novel The Gardens of Light by Amin Maalouf, which is about the life of Mani.

The image is public domain and can be found here
